notes: Ran absolute fine for me except bbuuttoonnss rreeggiisstteerr ttwwiiccee making running and interacting with things very difficult.
title: Rust

notes:
Game starts and work excellently, without any workarounds or tweaks. Performance is good and maximal settings, audio and input works fine in game, and graphics looks almost perfect. Perfectly playable overall.

There are few minor graphical glitches that put it away from Platinum: some textures are overlapping sometimes, and when cutting trees, a removed bark is black in shaded areas. There are few other minor areas where graphics looks glitch y (some water sometimes at beaches, and highlights when upgradeding building structures like walls, when looking at specific angles), but it is possible this is normal, as I can't compare it to windows behavior at the moment.

Also keyboard in chat emits two characters for every key press, sSoo  yyoouu ttaallkk lliikkee tthhiiss.
title: Rust

cpu: AMD A8-5600K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
title: Rust

notes: Runs flawlessly, to disable the double input bug associated with GNOME desktop. You need to add the custom launch option on steam: "XMODIFIERS='' %command%." without quotations of course.
title: Rust

notes:
Using the Launch Option "XMODIFIERS='' %command%" resolves the double-typing issue and game is largely playable. FPS in the high 70s to 80s at 1440p. However, the one issue I have keeping this from a Gold rating is that the shadows on the trees (usually only the ones at a distance) flicker every 1-2 seconds. Really, really annoying.

That being said, game runs great otherwise with no crashes and good framerate!
title: Rust

notes: This game runs natively on linux and does not require proton to run.
